description:
Issue at `internal/issue/issue.go:18–37` is a closed struct. Default json unmarshal silently drops unknown keys, so any downstream consumer writing domain-specific fields (e.g., review-type tickets carrying `annotations`, `index`, `reviewer`) loses them the moment `bw update` or any bw-mediated edit touches the ticket.

**Fix:** Add `Extra map[string]json.RawMessage` to Issue. Implement custom `UnmarshalJSON` that dumps keys outside the known set into Extra, and `MarshalJSON` that splats Extra back out alongside known fields. Collisions (a key appearing in both Extra and known fields) should be a unit-tested error or a documented precedence.

**Acceptance:**
- Round-trip test: unmarshal a JSON blob with `annotations: [...]` + known fields → marshal → byte-equal to input (modulo key ordering).
- `bw update --status closed` on a ticket carrying `annotations` preserves the array.
- Unit test for collision between a future known field and an existing Extra entry.

**Convention note** (no code — documented in `docs/` or AGENTS.md): downstream consumers should namespace their extensions (e.g. `extensions.octopus.*`) to avoid colliding with future beadwork-owned field names.

Priority 1: unblocks every downstream consumer.
title: Preserve unknown fields on Issue round-trip — lossless JSON
